title: Object literals
description: Key-value pairs. The workhorse data structure.

questions.prompt: Which is a valid object literal?
questions.explanation: Keys can be unquoted (if valid identifiers) or quoted strings. = is not valid — use ':'.
You can add properties later. Object.keys(user) is ['name', 'age'].

questions.prompt: The shorthand `{ name }` inside an object literal is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: ES6 shorthand: when the key and variable name match, drop the value.
